Features._comment: DevEndpoints stays false even in Development — the operator opts in explicitly via env var Features__DevEndpoints__Enabled=true. When off, /dev/* routes return 404.
Features._comment_swagger: Swagger stays false here too. Note: in the Development environment Swagger is served OPEN (no flag, no auth) by the IsDevelopment() branch in Program.cs — this flag only governs the admin-gated branch that runs under Production/Staging.

_comment_superlogin: Super-login (basic): POST /api/User/user-id-login with {userId, superAdminPassCode}. The UM validates superAdminPassCode against SuperAdmin:PassCode config. Inject the passcode via SuperAdmin__PassCode env var or dotnet user-secrets. The returned authToken carries the admin role claim.
_comment_tokenmint: Super-login+ (enhanced): POST /auth/tokens with {userId, roles?}. When Security:TokenMint:Enabled is false the gate is OPEN for local dev — any caller can mint a gateway-signed JWT for any userId without presenting X-Service-Auth-Key. This IS the correct enabled state for dev. In production/staging set Enabled=true and inject Security__TokenMint__Key via a swarm secret.
